title: --uidmap fails with `EOF: OCI runtime error`
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running a container with `--uidmap 0:0:1001` fails. Possibly because the range overlaps with more than one uidmap on the system.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run --rm --uidmap=0:0:1001 -u 1000 debian id -u`

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: container_linux.go:370: starting container process caused: process_linux.go:338: getting the final child's pid from pipe caused: EOF: OCI runtime error`

**Describe the results you expected:**

`1000`

Or, at least a better error message.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This works:

```
$ podman run --rm --uidmap=0:0:1 --uidmap=1000:1000:1 -u 1000 debian id -u
1000
```

My goal is to emulate `--userns=keep-id`, which is failing with the nvidia-container hook.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Tue Sep 29 21:00:00 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
You are right, the issue is that the specified new mappings overlap on two mappings in the current user namespace.  That error is coming directly from the kernel.

Not sure why it is done this way in the kernel, probably to simplify the implementation
Does this mean this is unfixable, other then to improve the error message?

> Does this mean this is unfixable, other then to improve the error message?

we could fix it if we take care of splitting the specified mappings, possibly in the OCI runtime, but I think it is better to try to fix it in the kernel first.  @ebiederm what do you think?

The error message above is generated by runc.  With crun it is:

```
$ podman run --rm --uidmap=0:0:1001 fedora true
Error: writing file `/proc/553298/gid_map`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I've proposed a patch for the kernel: https://lkml.kernel.org/lkml/20201126100839.381415-1-gscrivan@redhat.com/
@giuseppe isn't the PR that you just opened to related to this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
the kernel patch is stuck on review, but it is fixed now in Podman so we can close the issue here
